title: smallanthus araucariophilus
intro: Smallanthus araucariophilus, also known as Yerba Santa, is a perennial herb native to North America that belongs to the sunflower family. Traditionally, Yerba Santa has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and expectorant properties, making it beneficial for treating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The plant has been used to promote overall health and well-being, with its health benefits including relief from bronchitis, asthma, and other respiratory conditions. Yerba Santa is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and can be used to make herbal remedies for various purposes, including skin care and respiratory health.
